signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Disadvantage
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) vs USD -0.872% (n=7) sets up a strong bilateral theme. The 196bp US-JGB 10Y yield gap (US 4.38% vs JGB 2.42%) favors USD, running against JPY momentum. However, JPY COT net short -88.5k (30.6% long) indicates crowded short positioning, setting up potential short-squeeze risk. The yield disadvantage for JPY is likely to limit its upside, but current momentum and the G8 spread of 1.953% suggest a continued JPY bid. The next BoJ meeting on 28 Apr may provide further clarity on yield dynamics.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Tariff Escalation and ECB Hold
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723. Trump's tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16. EUR COT net short -18.3k reinforces the downside bias. The ECB's Hold stance at 2.00% and next meeting on 30 Apr may not provide enough support to counter the tariff impact. A break below 1.16 is likely to trigger further downside.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias and Crowded Long
signals.text: AUD COT net long +48.3k (74.8% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk. AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday. The RBA at 4.10% with a hike bias (40% priced) sustains the bid, but any disappointment could trigger an unwind. The RBA's next meeting on 5 May is the key catalyst for determining AUD's direction.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold and Cross-Asset Correlation
signals.text: GBP/USD closed at 1.3575. The BoE's Hold stance at 3.75% and next meeting on 30 Apr may keep the pair range-bound. The positive correlation between GBP/USD and FTSE 100, with a correlation of +0.327, suggests that the pair may follow the equity market's lead.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Fed Hold and Oil Price Impact
signals.text: USD/CAD closed at 1.3588. The Fed's Hold stance at 3.75% and next meeting on 29 Apr may keep the pair stable. The recent drop in WTI oil prices to $102.5 is likely to support the CAD, but the USD's overall strength is likely to limit the CAD's gains.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential (RBNZ 2.25% vs Fed 3.75%) favors the USD. However, specs are net short −16.8k (23.0% long), setting up a divergence with the RBNZ's hike bias, where 40% of a hike is priced. This creates a short-squeeze risk as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signaling. The unwind of these positions is likely to be catalyzed by the next RBNZ meeting on 27 May.
